title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
problem_statement: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree, where the path may start and end at any node and must follow parent-child connections.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 3 * 10^4]
intuition: Use post-order traversal to compute the maximum upward path through each node, while storing the best complete path that passes through that node.

approaches.name: Post-order recursion with global max
approaches.description: Recursively compute the max contribution of each child and update the maximum path sum that uses the current node as the highest node in the path.
approaches.why_not: Enumerating all possible node pairs is too slow for large trees.
interview_commentary: Ignore negative child contributions by clamping at zero.
The helper returns max gain extending upward from the current node.
Update maxSum with left + node + right to capture a full path passing through the node.

interview_qa.q: Why do we clamp negative child returns to zero?
interview_qa.a: Negative contributions reduce the sum, so stopping the path before them gives a better maximum.

interview_qa.q: What does the helper function return?
interview_qa.a: The maximum path sum starting at the current node and extending downward in one direction.

interview_qa.q: Why is the global max updated with left + node + right?
interview_qa.a: That represents the best path that passes through the current node as the highest point.

interview_qa.q: Can the maximum path be a single node?
interview_qa.a: Yes, if all child contributions are negative, the best path may consist of the node alone.
